title: Roasted Sick-Day Ethiopian Duck Legs Shiro
description: A pillowy, comforting Ethiopian dish featuring roasted duck legs, served in a flavorful shiro sauce.
instructions: Season the duck legs with salt, pepper, and berbere spice.
Heat oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat.
Sear the duck legs until browned on both sides, about 5 minutes per side.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and roast the duck legs for 25-30 minutes, or until cooked through.
While the duck legs are roasting, sauté onions and garlic in a separate pan until softened.
Add berbere spice and cook for an additional minute.
Deglaze the pan with a mixture of chicken broth and red wine, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan.
Stir in the shiro sauce and cook until heated through.
Shred the duck legs into bite-sized pieces and add to the shiro sauce.
Simmer the sauce over low heat until the flavors have melded together.
Serve the duck legs in the shiro sauce over injera or rice.
Garnish with chopped fresh cilantro, if desired.
